skill.name: python-testing-patterns
skill.description: Implement comprehensive testing strategies with pytest, fixtures, mocking, and test-driven development. Use when writing Python tests, setting up test suites, or implementing testing best practices.
skill.summary: Comprehensive guide to Python testing with pytest including fixtures, mocking, parameterization, and TDD patterns.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 87 static findings are false positives. The detected patterns (external_commands, network, env_access) are documentation examples of pytest commands, HTTP mocking patterns, and test fixture configurations - all legitimate testing education content. No actual security threats present.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Commands Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: Documentation showing pytest command examples like 'pytest test_example.py' in code blocks - educational content, not actual execution
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: These are markdown code blocks showing test commands, not shell execution calls

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Mocking Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: Test examples showing how to mock HTTP requests using unittest.mock - legitimate testing patterns
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Uses patch() to mock requests library - standard pytest testing practice

dangerous_patterns.title: Test Fixture Configurations
dangerous_patterns.description: Example test fixtures with fake/test values like 'test-key' and 'postgresql://localhost/test' - test data only
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Clearly marked as example/test values, not real credentials

content.value_statement: Writing maintainable tests is challenging. This skill provides comprehensive pytest patterns including fixtures, mocking, parameterization, and TDD workflows to help you build reliable test suites.
actual_capabilities: Create pytest test files with proper structure using AAA pattern (Arrange, Act, Assert)
Write fixtures with different scopes (function, class, session, module) for setup and teardown
Mock external dependencies using unittest.mock patch decorators and context managers
Use pytest.mark.parametrize for testing multiple input combinations efficiently
Implement test-driven development workflows with red-green-refactor cycles
Set up integration tests for APIs and databases with proper isolation
limitations: Does not cover specific framework testing (Django, Flask, FastAPI specific test tools)
No coverage of performance testing or load testing tools
Does not include security testing or penetration testing guidance
Limited coverage of GUI testing or web automation (selenium/playwright)

use_cases.title: New Python Project Testing Setup
use_cases.description: Set up a complete test suite for a new Python project with fixtures, conftest.py configuration, and CI/CD integration
use_cases.target_user: Developers starting new Python projects who need testing infrastructure

use_cases.title: Legacy Code Test Coverage
use_cases.description: Add tests to existing untested code using mocking to isolate units and parameterization to cover edge cases
use_cases.target_user: Engineers maintaining legacy Python codebases without tests

use_cases.title: API Integration Testing
use_cases.description: Write integration tests for REST APIs using fixtures for test data and mocking for external service calls
use_cases.target_user: Backend developers testing API integrations and service interactions

prompt_templates.title: Basic Test Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create pytest tests for a function called [FUNCTION_NAME] that [DESCRIPTION]. Use the AAA pattern.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to write unit tests for a simple function

prompt_templates.title: Fixture Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Create pytest fixtures with [SCOPE] scope to provide [RESOURCE] for tests. Include proper setup and teardown.
prompt_templates.scenario: When tests need shared resources like database connections or API clients

prompt_templates.title: Mocking External Calls
prompt_templates.prompt: Write tests that mock [EXTERNAL_SERVICE] calls using unittest.mock. Show both patch decorator and context manager approaches.
prompt_templates.scenario: When testing code that makes HTTP requests or calls external APIs

prompt_templates.title: Parameterized Testing
prompt_templates.prompt: Create parameterized tests for [FUNCTION] using pytest.mark.parametrize to test [EDGE_CASES].
prompt_templates.scenario: When testing a function with multiple input combinations or edge cases

output_examples.input: Write tests for a calculate_discount function that takes price and discount_percentage, returns discounted price
output_examples.output: Basic test file with multiple test cases covering normal discounts, zero discount, and invalid inputs

output_examples.input: Create fixtures for testing a database connection with session scope
output_examples.output: Fixture setup with proper yield/teardown pattern for database resource management

output_examples.input: Mock HTTP requests to an API client that fetches user data
output_examples.output: Tests using patch() to mock requests.get with different response scenarios

best_practices: Use descriptive test names that explain what is being tested and expected behavior
Keep tests independent with proper setup/teardown - no shared state between tests
Follow AAA pattern: Arrange (setup), Act (execute), Assert (verify) clearly separated
anti_patterns: Avoid test names like test_1 or test_function that do not describe the test purpose
Do not mix multiple assertions in one test when they could be separate tests
Avoid testing implementation details instead of behavior - test what the code does, not how

faq.question: What is the difference between unit tests and integration tests?
faq.answer: Unit tests test individual functions or classes in isolation. Integration tests test how multiple components work together, such as testing API endpoints with a real database.

faq.question: How do I mock a function that does not use requests library?
faq.answer: Use unittest.mock.patch decorator or context manager to replace any function or class with a Mock object. Import the function path as a string: @patch('module.function_name').

faq.question: What is fixture scope and when should I use each?
faq.answer: Function scope (default) creates fixture for each test. Class scope once per test class. Module scope once per module. Session scope once per entire test session. Use session/module for expensive resources.

faq.question: How do I test exceptions with pytest?
faq.answer: Use pytest.raises() as a context manager: with pytest.raises(ExpectedException): your_function(). Optionally pass match parameter to check exception message.

faq.question: What is test-driven development (TDD)?
faq.answer: TDD is a development approach where you write tests before code. Cycle: Write failing test (red), write minimal code to pass (green), refactor code (refactor). Helps ensure testable design.

faq.question: How do I run only tests matching a keyword?
faq.answer: Use pytest -k flag: pytest -k 'test_name_pattern' runs only tests with names matching the pattern. Use pytest -m marker to run tests with specific markers.
